title: Market Brief — Monday, April 27, 2026
summary: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 16% (70/296), H200 at 16% (56/324), and B200 at 19% (137/368), all well below the 25% scarcity threshold. H100 spot jumped 7.1% day-over-day to $2.00/hr, the sharpest move in the stack, while H200 and B200 both softened roughly 2.3-2.4%, signaling demand rotation toward legacy silicon for cost-sensitive inference workloads.
paragraphs: Compute is scarce across all GPU classes. H100 availability sits at 16% (70/296), H200 at 16% (56/324), and B200 at 19% (137/368), all well below the 25% scarcity threshold. H100 spot jumped 7.1% day-over-day to $2.00/hr, the sharpest move in the stack, while H200 and B200 both softened roughly 2.3-2.4%, signaling demand rotation toward legacy silicon for cost-sensitive inference workloads.

B200 is the only GPU where mid-tier inference operators break even on Llama 70B at TCO-adjusted cost of $3.69/M tokens, yielding a slim 2% margin at medium pricing. H100 and H200 operators selling at medium tier are burning cash, with H200 losing roughly $7.93/M tokens at that tier. Among API providers, DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output is priced at floor alongside open-source, grade: Burning Cash. OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output: Strong. Anthropic Opus 4.6 at $25/M: Neutral. Google Gemini 2.5 Pro at $10/M: Neutral.

B200 is undervalued at $4.03/hr spot versus $4.95 OCPI fair value, a 19% discount with utilization surging 43.9pp in 30 days, making spot accumulation the clearest trade on the board. H200 looks overvalued with 74% annualized volatility, scarce availability offering no liquidity cushion, and spot already trading above TCO-justified inference margins at all but premium tiers.
bullets: 16% of H100 GPUs are available, with only 70 of 296 units free on spot markets.
19% B200 availability across 368 GPUs marks scarcity despite the fastest utilization ramp at 81.2%.
$0.378 per million output tokens makes DeepSeek V3.2 the cheapest frontier-adjacent model, priced 99.5% below GPT-4 launch baseline.
74% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the riskiest compute asset to hold unhedged.
$975 per DDR5 32GB RDIMM reflects a 4.84% weekly spike, the steepest memory move in the stack.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means frontier output token costs have fallen 79% since GPT-4 launch.
